articles.title: Mumbai Mayor issues heat alert advice on hydration and sun safety - The News Mill
articles.full_text:
Mumbai Mayor Ritu Tawde urged residents to remain hydrated and avoid unnecessary sun exposure as several districts in Maharashtra face a heat alert. Speaking after the 23rd Maharashtra Mayor Council meeting on Sunday, she stated, “The heatwave has intensified significantly, and we are focusing on the overall situation. I urge people to drink as much water as possible, avoid sun exposure, and take care of their health. It is very important for people to remain vigilant.”
Tawde highlighted that the Mayor Council meeting, held in Mumbai after four and a half years, went successfully. “After four and a half years, elections for the municipal corporations have been held across Maharashtra. After such a long wait, the Maharashtra Mayors’ Council meeting concluded very successfully today. Mayors from all over Maharashtra are here. This was the first formal meeting of the council where I served as the President. Today, I took over as President, and Neeta Thakre, the honourable mayor of Nagpur, was declared the Vice President. Along with this, various issues related to their respective municipal corporations were discussed,” she said.
A yellow alert has been issued for Mumbai, Thane, Raigad, Konkan, and the Vidarbha region due to rising temperatures and heatwave-like conditions. Tawde noted, “While heatwaves are a natural seasonal phenomenon, public health precautions are essential to prevent heat-related illnesses.”
She added, “Rain is expected by the second week of June, but people must remain alert and take precautions.” The alert comes amid a persistent rise in temperatures across India, which has increasingly affected daily life. Many residents are staying indoors to avoid exposure to the intense heat.
Those obliged to venture outdoors for work or essential activities are adopting measures such as using umbrellas, wearing protective clothing, and regularly consuming cold beverages and seasonal fruits to manage dehydration and fatigue caused by the ongoing heatwave-like conditions.
